old_claim.note: Roads provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Military standards are visual symbols or ensigns; early symbolic communication is broad representational context rather than a direct technical production dependency.

source_shape.source_locator: Opening definition gives signa militaria as military ensigns or standards; later sections describe scarlet flags and standards as visual signs used to signal battle movement.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports military standards and scarlet flags as visual signs used in troop movement and battle signalling.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A visual standard is a symbolic communication device; roads may move armies but do not enable the representational signal itself.

ontology_before: Military Signal Flags depended on roads, implying transport infrastructure enabled the visual signal system.
ontology_after: Military Signal Flags depend on organized military formations plus symbolic communication; roads remain available for logistics and movement nodes.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: military_signal_flags now depends on early_symbolic_communication and no longer directly depends on roads.
would_reject_if: The node were broadened to courier, road-post, or logistics signaling rather than standards and visual signs.
A source showed the earliest scoped military standard signalling required roads rather than troop formations and visual symbols.
The node were split into Roman standards, Greek naval flags, and smoke/torch beacon systems with distinct prerequisites.
short_reason: Military standards are visual symbols; roads are logistics context, not the signal mechanism.
